steps.title: Identify the conversion factors
steps.content: We need to convert 5.70 m/s² to miles/min². Given: 1 mile = 1,609 meters. Additionally, since 1 minute = 60 seconds, we have 1 minute² = 3,600 second².

steps.title: Multiply by conversion factors
steps.content:
Multiply the acceleration by the distance and time conversion factors:

$$5.70 \text{ m/s}^2 \times \frac{1 \text{ mile}}{1,609 \text{ m}} \times \frac{3,600 \text{ s}^2}{1 \text{ min}^2}$$

The first fraction converts meters to miles, and the second converts second² to minute².

steps.title: Calculate the result
steps.content: Combine the conversion factors: $$= 5.70 \times \frac{3,600}{1,609}$$ Compute the numerator: $5.70 \times 3,600 = 20,520$. Divide: $20,520 \div 1,609 = 12.753...$ Rounded to the nearest tenth: 12.8 miles per minute squared.
